acceptance_notes: Evidence shows strong baseline performance: 81.45 mean score, 91pp R0-to-best gain, no catastrophic failures
Main weakness is boundary ambiguity with artifact-generation: 5 low-margin cases (0pp margin), 1 cross-schema loss (21pp gap on civ6-adjacency-optimizer)
Operations sharpen when/why this schema wins without rewriting working guidance; focus is differentiation, not capability fixes
Task profile signals support tightening: 4/5 staged-multi-step, 4/5 pipeline primary pattern — schema should own this cluster more decisively

challenger_guidance.conservative: Retain current schema; monitor artifact-generation boundary cases and adjust expected_bad_fit if low-margin assignments persist
challenger_guidance.differentiating: Emphasize quality-cascade property in meta_prompt opening; add explicit decision rule: 'If intermediate stage errors would cause final-stage failures invisible to the verifier until late in execution, this schema owns the task'
challenger_guidance.exploratory: Test a merged artifact-generation + analytic-pipeline schema with an explicit quality-cascade gate to unify borderline cases

expected_effect: Clearer boundary with artifact-generation reduces 0-margin assignments and prevents over-matching on single-artifact tasks
Quality-cascade emphasis strengthens schema identity and helps future assignment decisions distinguish true pipelines from artifact workflows
Explicit bad-fit exclusions prevent generator task mismatches like court-form-filling while preserving strong pipeline wins like earthquake-phase-association

add: quality-cascade property as the core differentiator vs artifact-generation
explicit boundary rule: prefer when intermediate output quality affects downstream correctness
exclude single-artifact tasks where only final output verification matters
exclude: single-artifact generation where all verification is final-output-only
keep: stage decomposition and stage ordering emphasis
final output tied back to pipeline evidence
core meta_prompt structure emphasizing staged workflows
sharpen: intermediate artifacts emphasis to focus on quality-dependent handoffs
good-fit criteria to emphasize error propagation from early stages
bad-fit to exclude tasks where stage ordering is arbitrary

add: treating single-artifact generation as a pipeline when only the final output contract matters
stage decomposition when no intermediate validation adds verifier value or catches upstream errors
the quality-cascade property: intermediate processing quality determines downstream correctness
explicit schema boundary: choose this when stage output quality matters for later stages, not just final artifact structure
extract task-specific stage dependencies and failure modes before adding generic pipeline scaffolding

sharpen.new: explicit intermediate validation when downstream stages depend on upstream output quality or correctness

add: single-artifact tasks where all verification occurs on the final output only
form-filling or template tasks where stage ordering is arbitrary or cosmetic
multi-stage workflows where intermediate processing errors propagate to final output quality
tasks whose verifier can fail due to early-stage mistakes even when late-stage logic is correct
boundary ambiguity with artifact-generation when both schemas fit but artifact-generation workflow is more natural for single-output tasks
over-matching on generator tasks that have review steps but no quality-cascade between stages
